agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have presented directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands no location sharing due to driver retention concerns.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Shipment Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person fulfilling a delivery order, also a key stakeholder whose privacy and retention are business-critical."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The recipient of a delivery who wants visibility into its progress."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The GPS-derived position of a courier, updated frequently during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to deliver a specific order to a customer."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived, privacy-preserving proxy for courier location that communicates delivery progress without exposing exact coordinates."},{"term":"Geofence zone","definition":"A defined geographic radius around the delivery destination used to trigger proximity-based notifications (e.g., 'Your courier is nearby') without revealing exact position."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The operational metric tracking courier workforce stability; a direct business cost when couriers leave the platform."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (traffic, break) — exact GPS would show them not moving, triggering customer complaints and driver anxiety.","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core stakeholder contradiction."},{"scenario":"Courier is multi-dropping (delivering multiple orders on one route) — exact GPS reveals they are not heading directly to this customer.","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core stakeholder contradiction."},{"scenario":"Courier's personal location after delivery completion — if tracking persists beyond the delivery window, it becomes a serious privacy violation.","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core stakeholder contradiction."},{"scenario":"Courier is in a sensitive location (hospital, shelter) between deliveries.","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS must never be exposed outside an active delivery window, regardless of which policy is chosen."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"This conflict has direct P&L implications on both sides. Customer Success argues transparency drives NPS and reduces churn (revenue protection). Operations argues driver attrition is increasing recruitment and training costs (cost inflation). Neither team has presented data to quantify their claim, which is itself a problem. Driver replacement cost in logistics typically runs $3,000–$5,000 per driver; customer churn from poor tracking UX is real but harder to attribute solely to GPS granularity.","operational":"If we ship exact GPS: driver attrition accelerates, route capacity shrinks, delivery SLAs degrade — a vicious cycle. If we ship nothing: customer support ticket volume for 'where is my order?' remains high, WISMO calls consume agent time. The operational cost of the contradiction itself is the biggest risk — shipping either extreme damages the business.","user":"Customers want certainty their delivery is coming. Couriers want dignity and autonomy. These are not inherently incompatible — the contradiction is in the proposed solutions, not the underlying needs.","risk":"CRITICAL: Shipping exact GPS coordinates without driver consent may violate GDPR Article 6 (lawful basis), employee monitoring regulations in multiple jurisdictions, and potentially local labor laws. This has not been reviewed by Legal. Additionally, a data breach exposing real-time courier locations creates physical safety risks for drivers.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery certainty. Exact GPS is one solution but not the only one. ETA, progress stages, and proximity alerts may satisfy the actual need."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected. Exact GPS sharing creates surveillance perception, reduces autonomy, and is cited as a retention driver. Their physical safety is also at risk if location data is breached or misused."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns NPS and retention metrics. Believes GPS transparency is a differentiator but may be conflating 'delivery visibility' with 'exact coordinates.'"},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply and delivery capacity. Losing drivers directly degrades the ability to fulfill orders."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"NOT YET CONSULTED. Real-time employee location sharing to third parties has significant regulatory implications (GDPR, labor law, data protection). This is a major gap."},{"group":"Product / Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. The decision encodes a strategic priority: supply-side (driver) health vs. demand-side (customer) experience."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Confidence is 40 because the spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between two authoritative stakeholder teams, AND a critical stakeholder (Legal) has not been consulted on what may be a regulated activity. I cannot define correct behavior when the business has not decided what correct means. Additionally, the Customer Success team's requirement ('exact GPS coordinates at all times') may not survive legal review in many jurisdictions, which could render the entire framing moot.","high_confidence_areas":["The underlying customer need is delivery certainty, not necessarily exact GPS","Driver privacy and retention is a legitimate operational constraint with real cost","Legal/compliance review is mandatory before any location-sharing feature ships","The two stated requirements are genuinely contradictory and cannot both be satisfied as written"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder priority the business will choose","Whether legal constraints will eliminate one or both options","What granularity of location data actually moves customer satisfaction metrics","Whether drivers would accept a compromise (e.g., proximity zones) or reject any tracking"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and representing real signals from their constituencies","No legal review has been conducted on sharing courier GPS with customers","The business does not yet have A/B test data showing the impact of GPS granularity on customer satisfaction or driver retention"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from peer-level stakeholder teams. Neither has been designated as the priority by executive leadership. I cannot produce valid acceptance criteria until this is resolved.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success","Operations","Legal","Executive Leadership"],"options":["A: Ship exact GPS per Customer Success — accept driver attrition risk and legal exposure","B: Ship no location data per Operations — accept higher WISMO support volume and potential customer churn","C: Ship a privacy-preserving compromise (ETA + delivery stages + geofence proximity alerts) that addresses the underlying needs of both teams without exact GPS — requires both teams to accept that their stated requirement was a solution, not the actual need","D: Run a controlled pilot with tiered visibility (ETA-only vs. proximity zone vs. exact GPS) and measure both customer NPS and driver sentiment before committing"],"recommendation":"Option D first (data-driven pilot), with Option C as the likely long-term architecture. The Customer Success team's actual need is 'delivery certainty,' not 'exact coordinates.' The Operations team's actual need is 'driver dignity and retention,' not 'zero visibility.' A proximity-based model (courier is nearby, courier is 2 stops away, ETA 5 minutes) likely satisfies both. But this is a BUSINESS DECISION that must be made by executive leadership with Legal input — I am flagging it, not making it."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and compliance review has not been conducted. Sharing real-time GPS coordinates of employees/contractors with third-party customers is a regulated activity in many jurisdictions (GDPR, CCPA, labor monitoring laws). This review MUST happen before any acceptance criteria can be finalized, as legal constraints may eliminate one or more options entirely.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal","Couriers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/DPO for a data protection impact assessment before defining requirements","Proceed without legal review and accept regulatory risk"],"recommendation":"Engage Legal immediately. Do not ship any courier location feature without a completed DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment). This is non-negotiable from a compliance standpoint."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot accept Customer Success requirement as stated: 'exact GPS coordinates at all times' directly contradicts Operations requirement and has not been reviewed for legal compliance.","business_rule_broken":"No business rule can be defined when two authoritative stakeholders present mutually exclusive requirements."},{"violation":"Cannot accept Operations requirement as stated: 'never show location' may be an overreaction that sacrifices legitimate customer value. The actual driver concern (surveillance) can likely be addressed without zero visibility.","business_rule_broken":"No business rule can be defined when two authoritative stakeholders present mutually exclusive requirements."}]}
